event_description: An airborne camera observes a small, yellow single-engine aircraft (likely an agricultural or light utility plane) taxiing on an airport apron. Initially, severe solar glare and overexposure in the EO view obscure the target almost entirely, while the IR view shows a low-contrast thermal signature against the sun-baked concrete. As the aircraft moves and the camera angle shifts, the yellow plane becomes clearly visible in EO, and its active engine/exhaust hotspot becomes distinct in IR.

qa.question: In the early EO segment, which cue pairing is visible for the object?

options.A: the object with taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; sun-baked concrete and glare nearby
options.B: the object with sun-baked concrete and glare; taxiing aircraft emerging from glare nearby
options.C: the object with runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; sun glare or paved-surface texture nearby
options.D: the object with sun glare or paved-surface texture; taxiing or ground-motion cue nearby

qa.evidence_note: the plane is initially obscured by glare, then becomes visible as it taxis

qa.question: Across the EO scale or angle change, which motion cue stays paired with the object?

options.A: sun-baked concrete and glare; view scale or angle changes
options.B: runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; view scale or angle changes
options.C: sun glare or paved-surface texture; view scale or angle changes
options.D: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; view scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the plane is initially obscured by glare, then becomes visible as it taxis

qa.question: In IR, which contrast pairing is visible near the object?

options.A: aircraft contrast against heated pavement; runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region
options.B: aircraft contrast against heated pavement; sun-baked concrete and glare
options.C: aircraft contrast against heated pavement; taxiing aircraft emerging from glare
options.D: aircraft contrast against heated pavement; sun glare or paved-surface texture

qa.evidence_note: a low-contrast signature and later engine or exhaust hotspot track the active aircraft

qa.question: Which nearby cue creates a same-scene confusion?

options.A: sun glare or paved-surface texture; near the object
options.B: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; near the object
options.C: runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; near the object
options.D: sun-baked concrete and glare; near the object

qa.evidence_note: using the early glare interval as evidence that no aircraft is moving

qa.question: Which EO/IR cue pair is visible?

options.A: EO: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; IR: sun glare or paved-surface texture
options.B: EO: runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; IR: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.C: EO: sun-baked concrete and glare; IR: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.D: EO: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; IR: aircraft contrast against heated pavement

qa.evidence_note: EO: the plane is initially obscured by glare, then becomes visible as it taxis; IR: a low-contrast signature and later engine or exhaust hotspot track the active aircraft

qa.question: Which cue pairing continues through camera/view change?

options.A: the object: sun-baked concrete and glare; screen scale or angle changes
options.B: the object: sun glare or paved-surface texture; screen scale or angle changes
options.C: the object: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; screen scale or angle changes
options.D: the object: runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; screen scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the plane is initially obscured by glare, then becomes visible as it taxis

qa.question: Which EO cue is aligned with the IR contrast cue?

options.A: EO cue: runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; IR cue: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.B: EO cue: sun glare or paved-surface texture; IR cue: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.C: EO cue: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; IR cue: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.D: EO cue: sun-baked concrete and glare; IR cue: aircraft contrast against heated pavement

qa.evidence_note: EO: the plane is initially obscured by glare, then becomes visible as it taxis; IR: a low-contrast signature and later engine or exhaust hotspot track the active aircraft

qa.question: Which competing cue appears in the cross-modal chain?

options.A: EO/visible cue: sun-baked concrete and glare; IR/contrast context: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.B: EO/visible cue: runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; IR/contrast context: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.C: EO/visible cue: sun glare or paved-surface texture; IR/contrast context: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.D: EO/visible cue: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; IR/contrast context: aircraft contrast against heated pavement

qa.evidence_note: using the early glare interval as evidence that no aircraft is moving

qa.question: When the view changes, which cue is checked against the nearby alternative?

options.A: sun-baked concrete and glare; taxiing aircraft emerging from glare
options.B: sun glare or paved-surface texture; taxiing aircraft emerging from glare
options.C: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; sun-baked concrete and glare
options.D: runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; sun-baked concrete and glare

qa.evidence_note: the plane is initially obscured by glare, then becomes visible as it taxis

qa.question: Which IR cue pairing appears in this interval?

options.A: aircraft contrast against heated pavement; sun-baked concrete and glare
options.B: aircraft contrast against heated pavement; runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region
options.C: aircraft contrast against heated pavement; taxiing aircraft emerging from glare
options.D: aircraft contrast against heated pavement; sun glare or paved-surface texture

qa.evidence_note: a low-contrast signature and later engine or exhaust hotspot track the active aircraft

qa.question: Which combined EO/IR sequence account is visible?

options.A: EO: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; IR: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.B: EO: sun glare or paved-surface texture; IR: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.C: EO: runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; IR: aircraft contrast against heated pavement
options.D: EO: sun-baked concrete and glare; IR: aircraft contrast against heated pavement

qa.evidence_note: EO: the plane is initially obscured by glare, then becomes visible as it taxis; IR: a low-contrast signature and later engine or exhaust hotspot track the active aircraft

qa.question: For the full clip, which cue chain is visible?

options.A: the object: sun-baked concrete and glare; nearby cue: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare
options.B: the object: runway, taxiway, apron, or glare region; nearby cue: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare
options.C: the object: taxiing aircraft emerging from glare; nearby cue: sun-baked concrete and glare
options.D: the object: sun glare or paved-surface texture; nearby cue: sun-baked concrete and glare

qa.evidence_note: using the early glare interval as evidence that no aircraft is moving

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
